rules.title: Link references missing route
rules.description: A Next.js <Link> component references a route path that has no corresponding page.tsx or page.js file in the app directory. The link will lead to a 404.
rules.impact: Users click a link that leads to a 404 page.
rules.suggestedFix: Create the missing page.tsx/page.js file for the referenced route, or fix the href to point to an existing route.

rules.title: Server action missing 'use server' directive
rules.description: A function that appears to be a server action (uses cookies, headers, redirect, revalidatePath, or database calls) is defined without the 'use server' directive. Without it, the function runs on the client and will fail.
rules.impact: Server-only APIs called on client will throw runtime errors or silently fail.
rules.suggestedFix: Add 'use server' at the top of the file or at the beginning of the async function body.

rules.title: Client component imports server-only module
rules.description: A file marked with 'use client' imports a module that should only run on the server (fs, prisma, database, crypto for Node, server-only). This will cause a build error or runtime crash.
rules.impact: Build failure or runtime crash when client bundle tries to include server-only code.
rules.suggestedFix: Move server-only logic to a separate server component or API route. Use 'server-only' package to enforce boundaries.

rules.title: Middleware matcher may not cover protected routes
rules.description: The middleware.ts config.matcher pattern doesn't appear to cover routes that have authentication checks, meaning some protected pages may be accessible without middleware-level auth.
rules.impact: Protected routes may be accessible without middleware-level authentication or authorization.
rules.suggestedFix: Ensure middleware.config.matcher includes all routes that require authentication.

rules.title: Dynamic route missing loading or error boundary
rules.description: A dynamic route segment (e.g. [slug], [id]) has no loading.tsx or error.tsx sibling. Dynamic routes fetch data and are prone to loading states and errors that need graceful handling.
rules.impact: Users see raw loading flicker or unhandled error screens on dynamic pages.
rules.suggestedFix: Add loading.tsx and error.tsx files in the dynamic route directory.
